MINI MINUTES: Judge Pro Tem Will Chen, Clerk Carla Amador, Not Reported. (218/206)

Appearances:
Plaintiff Helen Yung is present.
Attorney, John Michael Phelps, representing the plaintiff.

Defendant Anthony Sagrillo AKA T.K. Sagrillo is present.
Attorney, Michael R. Bracamontes, representing the defendant.

This matter comes on calendar for an Unlawful Detainer Settlement Conference. The Judge Pro Tem, litigants and counsel confer privately for settlement discussions.

This case did not settle, the trial date of October 22, 2007 remains on calendar.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-16-2007 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Michael R. Bracamontes, Esq. of Bradshaw & Associates (415) 433-4800, appearing for defendant/moving party Anthony Sagrillo
J. Michael Phelps, Esq. (415) 433-3733, appearing for plaintiff/opposing party Helen Yung

RULING:
DEFENDANT ANTHONY SAGRILLO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT on plaintiff's second amended complaint. ARGUED and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows -- DEFENDANT ANTHONY SAGRILLO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. THERE IS AT LEAST A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT AS TO WHETHER PLAINTIFF HAS OBTAINED THE NECESSARY PERMITS TO REMOVE THE RENTAL UNIT FROM HOUSING USE. Prevailing party is to prepare a form of order comply with CCP 437 c(g). Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR #8189 =(301/PJB/PM)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-10-2007 Continued To Oct-16-07 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 301

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery And For An Award Of Attorney'S Fees And Sanctions;
Off Calendar Oct-10-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES- ATTY. J. MICHAEL PHELPS, TEL. NO. 433-3733 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
NO OTHER APPEARANCE ON THE MATTER.

RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED IT'S TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS - Defendant ANTHONY SAGRILLO'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery is moot as supplemental responses have been served. The court will not determine the adequacy of those responses on this motion. Counsel shall meet and confer. Counsel are advised that objections would not likely be found to have merit and that incorporation by reference is not a proper response. The court finds that the initial responses and late supplemental responses were a misuse of the discovery process. Sanctions are imposed against Plaintiff in the amount of $1,400, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
